MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT MERRILL LYNCH, PIERCE, FENNER & SMITH INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted.

(See Word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Plaintiff Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Levaggi Terms And Conditions Exhibits Submitted As Evidence In Defendant Motion To Compel Arbitration And All Supportive Papers Therewith
Off Calendar Apr-08-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF JAMI KATZ's Motion To Strike Levaggi Terms And Conditions Exhibits Submitted As Evidence In Defendant Motion To Compel Arbitration.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to strike is denied. The Court views this "motion" as a simple objection to defendant's evidence. Therefore, the Court rejects defendant's arguments that plaintiff failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure sections 435.5 and 1005. Defendant sufficiently establishes that the adduced records are properly authenticated and admissible under the business records exception to the hearsay rule. (See Evid. Code esc. 1271.) The trial court has broad discretion in admitting business records. (Estate of O'Connor (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 159, 169-171 [admitting account records].) Here, the Levaggi declaration properly authenticates the records. Levaggi establishes that he is a qualified witness (Levaggi Decl., pars. 2-3), the writings were made in the regular course of business at or near the time of the act (Id., par. 18), and the cited terms and conditions were part of the agreements. (Id., pars. 6, 9, and 16.) The matching codes on the account agreements and terms and conditions further indicate trustworthiness.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
